dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7251 IH: Prohibit Partisan Park Passes Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Elfreth (for herself, Ms. Ansari, Ms. Chu, Mr. Cleaver, Mr. Cohen, Ms. Davids of Kansas, Mr. Goldman of New York, Mr. Huffman, Mr. Lieu, Ms. Norton, Mr. Peters, Ms. Pingree, Mr. Quigley, Mr. Thanedar, Ms. Titus, and Mrs. Watson Coleman) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources, and in addition to the Committee on Agriculture, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned

form.official-title: To amend the Federal Lands Recreation Enhancement Act to prohibit the use of the image of living political figures on the America the Beautiful—the National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Prohibit Partisan Park Passes Act.

section.header: Restriction on use of images for Recreational Lands Pass
section.text: Section 805(a)(2) of the Federal Lands Recreation Enhancement Act (16 U.S.C. 6804(a)(2)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the heading, by striking competition; and

by adding at the end the following: The image used on the National Parks and Federal Recreational Lands Pass may not include a living current or former elected official or other living political figure..
